title: Sports Hub’s Mike Felger shares wild Red Sox trade rumor involving Mike Trout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Radio host Mike Felger discussed a rumor about Red Sox interest in trading for Angels' star Mike Trout.
Trade speculation includes Trout joining Boston in a deal involving Jarren Duran and Brayan Bello.
Trout, an 11-time All-Star, faces scrutiny due to age, injury history, and significant salary through 2030.

title: Romeo Doubs on learning Josh McDaniels’ offense: ‘I can already see how he thinks’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Romeo Doubs is transitioning from the Packers to the Patriots, adjusting to Josh McDaniels' offensive system.
Doubs appreciates Patriots quarterback Drake Maye's energy and looks forward to building rapport within the team.
Despite leading Packers in key statistical categories, Doubs is open to varied roles with the Patriots, including special teams.

title: DA: Mass. man walked into police station, told officers he killed his father
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: David Boucher, 48, charged with first degree murder for allegedly killing his father in North Adams, Massachusetts.
Boucher reportedly confessed to police a week after allegedly stabbing his father multiple times.
Boucher pleaded not guilty and is held without bail; pretrial hearing set for June 12.

title: Worcester man, 62, killed by vehicle while crossing street, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: James McGuire, 62, was fatally struck by a vehicle near 137 Hope Ave., Worcester, Sunday night.
McGuire was taken to a nearby hospital where he succumbed to his injuries after the accident.
Worcester police are investigating; details on the driver and potential charges have not been disclosed.

title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy suspended 6 games for slashing Sabres’ Zach Benson
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy suspended six games for slashing Sabres' Zach Benson during Game 6 at TD Garden.
NHL Department of Player Safety held an in-person hearing, making McAvoy's six-game suspension possible.
This is McAvoy's third NHL suspension, with previous bans in 2019 and 2023 for illegal checks to the head.

title: TD Bank doubles its space amid return-to-office push
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: TD Bank expands to 39,000 square feet at 2 International Place in Boston, doubling current office space.
The bank's new lease begins in 2027, accommodating 140 employees, with capacity for 170, over a 10-year term.
Boston office visits increased by 11.2% in March, still 34.3% below pre-pandemic levels, amid return-to-office trends.

title: What would AJ Brown bring to the Patriots if they land him? All-Pro DB Kevin Byard knows.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots are rumored to be interested in trading for Eagles receiver AJ Brown, according to Kevin Byard.
AJ Brown's presence would help open up the field for other players, providing options for the Patriots' offense.
AJ Brown has achieved over 1,000 yards in six of his first seven seasons, showcasing his consistent offensive impact.

title: Celebrate America’s most painted building in Rockport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Motif No. 1 Day, celebrating America's most painted building, is on May 16 in Rockport.
The event features a 5K race, live music, plein air painters, and local art demonstrations.
Built in 1884, Motif No. 1 was rebuilt after the Blizzard of 1978 and repainted in 2024.

title: Middleborough Dairy Queen sells for more than $4 million
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Dairy Queen in Middleborough sold for $4.2 million to owner George Pennini's nieces, Nicole Pennini Ferrara and Kara Pennini Kendrigan.
Middleborough Dairy Queen is the second-highest-grossing 'Treat Only' franchise and most-visited in Massachusetts.
The property, originally purchased by the Pennini family in 1966, will remain family-owned under the new management.

title: Kane’s Donuts debuts World Cup-themed doughnuts to crown fans’ favorite team
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Kane's Donuts launches a series of country-themed doughnuts for the World Cup, starting with Haiti and Scotland.
Each week, Kane's will release new doughnuts inspired by different countries playing in the tournament at Boston Stadium.
Kane's Donuts has locations in Saugus and Boston and aims to engage fans during the World Cup with themed treats.
